name: Snowflake
tagline: Snowflake lets you dynamically create recursive-descent parsers.
body:
**This site is still under construction.**

Snowflake is actually two projects in one, each of which are described below.

Snowflake is licensed under the [Apache 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html) licence.

**Part I:** The Library

The library facilitates the creation of PEG-based recursive-descent parsers at runtime.

Quick Links:
* [Download the library as a precompiled jar file.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/snowflake.jar)
* [Download the library source-code and unit-tests as a zip file.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/snowflake-library-latest.zip)
* [Browse the library source-code.](https://github.com/Mackenzie-High/snowflake/tree/master/snowflake-library)
* [View the Javadoc documentation for the library.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/javadoc/library/index.html)
* [View unit-test coverage statistics.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/latest-test-coverage-statistics.jpeg)

**Part II:** The Interactive Graphical Grammar Editor

The grammar editor provides you the ability to interactively develop PEG grammars.

Quick Links:
* [Getting Started](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Snowflake-GUI-Getting-Started.pdf)
* [Cheat Sheet](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Snowflake-GUI-Cheat-Sheet.pdf)
* [Download the grammar editor as a single precompiled jar file.](http://Mackenzie-High.github.io/snowflake/webapp/snowflake-gui.jar)
* [Download the grammar editor's source-code and unit-tests as a zip file.](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/snowflake-gui-latest.zip)

**Part III:** Downloadable Examples
* A Simple Boolean Expression Evaluator
* A Simple Phone Book Parser
* A Prefix Notation Calculator
* An Infix Notation Calculator

**Part IV:** Here are some other resources and links that may help you.
* [Wikipedia: Parsing Expression Grammars](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parsing_expression_grammar)
* [The Packrat Parsing and Parsing Expression Grammars Page](http://bford.info/packrat/)
* [Parsing Expression Grammars: An Example Driven Introduction](http://mackenzie-high.github.io/snowflake/other/Grammars.pdf)
